mnemonics.title: Rule of Three Song
mnemonics.content: Destructor, Copy Constructor, Assignment - if one you define, define all three for proper management!

mnemonics.title: Assignment Returns Reference
mnemonics.content: A.ssign R.eference E.nables C.haining (ASREC) - remember: A = B = C works because we return reference

acronyms.usage: Compiler optimization that avoids copying return values by constructing them directly in caller's memory
Idiomatic C++ pattern where resources are acquired in constructor and released in destructor, related to proper object lifecycle management
The destination object in an assignment operation (a = b; a is LHS)
The source object in an assignment operation (a = b; b is RHS)

visualTips.visualization: Draw two boxes (objects) with a stick (pointer) inside. Shallow copy: both boxes have sticks pointing to same external box. Deep copy: both boxes have their own sticks pointing to different external boxes.
Timeline: Object creation moment triggers copy constructor (new object). Existing object getting new value triggers assignment operator.
Draw a person (object) holding a mirror (this pointer) showing themselves. The mirror always reflects the current person.

analogies.analogy: Like photocopying a document with sticky notes - the notes (pointers) point to the same original locations, not independent copies.
Like transcribing a handwritten book - you create a completely new book with your own paper and ink, independent of the original.
Like pouring liquid from one jug (source) to another (destination) - the destination's old content is replaced, source remains unchanged.
Like using a cake recipe (template) to bake a new cake - you use an existing pattern to create something new.
